mechanism_narrative: A flat or falling FEDFUNDS rate sustains lower discount rates and easier liquidity conditions, which lifts equity valuations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when inflation is not accelerating sharply.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after non-increasing FEDFUNDS than after rising FEDFUNDS, or if the sign of the effect reverses when CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a recession-warning threshold signals weakening household income and demand, which compresses earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes with UNRATE above 9.0% do not produce negative median 20-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate of negative returns falls below 55% in the triggered sample.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous acceleration in HOUST and RSAFS identifies a broad demand upswing that improves cyclical revenue outlooks and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. The signal is not active if only one of the two series improves.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint improvement in HOUST and RSAFS does not outperform each series alone in forecasting 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal has a negative median return.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO above its recent trend indicates stronger industrial output and inventory rebuilding, which supports cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week. The signal weakens if output gains occur alongside deteriorating labor conditions.
failure_condition: Reject if positive short-horizon SPX returns do not follow above-trend INDPRO observations, or if the effect disappears when PAYEMS is simultaneously declining.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is rising and FEDFUNDS remains near zero, real policy rates are deeply negative, which supports risk assets through liquidity and discount-rate channels. Under that combination, S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises by more than 0.2% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS stays at or below 0.25%
failure_condition: Reject if the triggered low-real-rate inflation regime does not produce positive median 20-day SPX returns, or if returns are no better than unconditional baseline in the same sample.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE signals labor market deterioration that leads investors to cut earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect is strongest when both changes occur in the same monthly print sequence.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS declines and UNRATE increases do not predict negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power over either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in GDPC1 after a prior slowdown signals improving aggregate demand and revenue expectations, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is strongest when retail sales and housing are not simultaneously weakening.
failure_condition: Reject if GDP rebounds fail to coincide with above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the positive effect vanishes when RSAFS and HOUST are both below trend.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS rises from a near-zero base while CPIAUCSL remains elevated, markets infer an imminent policy tightening path that raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails if inflation is falling at the same time.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by at least 0.25 pp from the prior month while CPIAUCSL inflation remains above 2% year over year
failure_condition: Reject if triggered tightening episodes do not produce negative median 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect is absent when CPIAUCSL inflation remains elevated.
